calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-10-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Albert Kun for and with Raymond Barrier.

The Court noted his concern that the administrator had improperty distributed estate assets to himself without prior Court approval. Mr. Kun stated that Mr. Barrier had misunderstood his powers under paragraph 3 of the letters; that Mr. Barrier was the sole beneficiary of the estate, and that no one had been harmed by the unauthorized distribution. Mr. Barrier stated that one-half of the sales proceeds he had put into his trust constituted proceeds from the sle of his interest in the property, and that he had not realized he was doing anything wrong is placing all the proceeds into the trust. The Court commented that while Mr. Barrier did have the right as administrator to sell the property, it was improper for him to distribute the proceeds of sale without first obtaining court authorization. Turning to the issue of attorneys fees, the Court stated that he could not award fees in excess of those requested in the petition before the Court. Mr. Kun said he accepted that limitation. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-26-2016 Continued To Aug-09-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-12-2016 Continued To Jul-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-21-2016 Continued To Jul-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Albert Kun for petitioner Raymond Barrier.

Mr. Kun stated that at the last hearing there were only two issues which Judge Busch requested to be addressed, and Mr. Kun filed a supplemental declaration on June 16 which addressed both questions. He stated that all the money in the estate will go to the petitioner, Raymmond Barrier, who is 92 years old. In response to the Court's inquiry as to where those funds are now located, Mr. Kun stated that the money is in a Wells Fargo bank account and has not been distributed to the petitioner. The Court stated that he needed to continue the matter to give the probate examiner time to review the supplemental declaration, and noted that it might be necessary to file an amended petiton for fees, as Mr. Kun is entitled to a higher fee than sought under his original petition. The matter was continued to July 12, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: John Boncato. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-24-2016 Continued To Jun-21-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Albert Kun for (petitioner) Raymond Barrier.

The Court stated that this is a petition for final distribution, that the property has been sold and asked counsel where the proceeds of the sale are. Mr. Kun stated that the proceeds belong to the petitioner, that he was a 50% owner and the decedent was a 50% owner, that he has not distributed the money and that it is there, that the cash balance is in addition to the proceeds of the sale, that he was the only distributee, that it was sold at a private sale with the permission of the court and that he kept the money. The Court stated that he cannot do that without a court order, that if there is more cash in the estate we need to know about it, that if he has distributed it that was wrong and he was not allowed to do so, that the court's consent to sell the property did not include distribution and that the funds were to go to the estate. Mr. Kun stated that he will amend the petition. The Court stated that it will need an amendment or something before the court to explain what happened to the cash, that it is not clear if the statutory fee is being requested in the petition and that they need to clarify. The accounting petition was continued to June 21, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-10-2016 Continued To May-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-12-2016 Continued To May-10-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Albert Kun for (executor) Raymond Barrier.

Mr. Kun reported that this will take so much time to do again, that they paintings are not Picasso's but only paintings done by the decedent, that the recipients are elderly and all over the U.S. and that most are in retirement homes. The Court stated that what we do not have is a proof of service or a proposed order, that counsel is responding to the examiner's notes but that it needs to be done in writing and that the procedural issues need to be cured. Mr. Kun stated that he will need a 30-day continuance. The Court stated that it has continued this over and over for several months now, asked how hard is it to proved a proof of service or proposed order on a petition, to respond in writing to the notes, that the court will not listen to the responses today and that it will continue the matter 1 last time and for Mr. Kun to respond to the notes in writing at least a week in advance of the hearing date. The accounting petition was continued to May 10, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Albert Kun for (petitioner) Raymond Barrier.

Mr. Kun reported that he has the signed final petition ready to be filed. The Court stated that it will continue the matter one more time for the status of filing, that if it is filed the status will go off calendar and that the Order to Appear will be taken off calendar stating that Mr. Kun should have appeared at the last hearing. The status of filing a petition for final distribution was continued to January 20, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not Reported)

Petition For Preliminary Distribution And For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jun-02-2015 Continued To Jun-30-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Albert Kun for Raymond Barrier.

The Court stated that there has been no response to the examiner's notes. Mr. Kun stated that there is a problem with a gift to the City, that he had been waiting for a response from Ms. Freelander of the City Attorney's Office, is now working with another person in that office and has not heard back yet, that his client is 92 years old and eager to close this estate and that they will do so as soon as the City Attorney gives them direction. The Court stated that there were several issues that were not responded to. Mr. Kun reported that he was planning to address all issues at once, that they plan to gift the paintings to whoever wants them and that they will donate anything within reason. The Petition for Preliminary Distribution and for Attorneys Fees was continued to June 30, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and Mr. Kun was instructed to provide responses to the examiner's questions even if it is just to say he is waiting on an answer.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-06-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Albert Kun for Petitioner.

The lost will dated June 12, 1997 is admitted to probate. The Court informed Counsel that going forward this does not mean that notice is waived. Mr. Kun stated that they noticed everyone and that there is nothing more they can do. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond is set at $12,000.00.
